sections.label: A
sections.title: Multiple Choice Questions
sections.instruction: Choose the correct option. Each question carries 1 mark.

questions.text:
Which of the following cannot be the probability of an event?

A) 2/3
B) -1.5
C) 15%
D) 0.7
questions.answer: B) -1.5. The probability P(E) of an event E is always a real number such that 0 ≤ P(E) ≤ 1. Therefore, it cannot be negative.

questions.text:
If P(E) = 0.05, what is the probability of 'not E' (P(Ē))?

A) 0.95
B) 0.05
C) 0.50
D) 1.05

The sum of the probabilities of all the elementary events of an experiment is:

A) 0
B) 0.5
C) 1
D) 2
questions.answer: C) 1. By definition, the sum of the probabilities of all the elementary events of an experiment is 1.

questions.text:
A missing helicopter is reported to have crashed somewhere in a rectangular region of dimensions 9 km by 4.5 km. There is a lake of dimensions 3 km by 2.5 km in that region. What is the probability that it crashed inside the lake?

A) 5/27
B) 7.5/40
C) 2/9
D) 1/12
questions.answer: A) 5/27. The total area of the region is 9 km × 4.5 km = 40.5 km². The area of the lake is 3 km × 2.5 km = 7.5 km². The probability is Area of lake / Total area = 7.5 / 40.5 = 75 / 405 = 5/27.

sections.label: B
sections.title: Short Answer Questions
sections.instruction: Attempt all questions. Each question carries 2 marks.

questions.text:
A die is thrown once. Find the probability of getting:
(i) a prime number
(ii) an odd number
questions.answer:
The possible outcomes when a die is thrown once are {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}, so total number of outcomes = 6.
(i) Prime numbers are {2, 3, 5} (3 outcomes). P(prime number) = 3/6 = 1/2.
(ii) Odd numbers are {1, 3, 5} (3 outcomes). P(odd number) = 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.text:
Savita and Hamida are friends. What is the probability that both will have:
(i) different birthdays?
(ii) the same birthday? (ignoring a leap year)
questions.answer:
There are 365 days in a non-leap year. Let Savita's birthday be any day. 
(i) If Hamida's birthday is different from Savita's, the number of favorable outcomes for her birthday is 365 - 1 = 364. Hence, P(different birthdays) = 364/365.
(ii) P(same birthday) = 1 - P(different birthdays) = 1 - 364/365 = 1/365.

questions.text:
Two dice, one blue and one grey, are thrown at the same time. Find the probability that the sum of the two numbers appearing on top of the dice is:
(i) 8
(ii) less than or equal to 12
questions.answer:
The total number of possible outcomes when two dice are thrown is 6 × 6 = 36.
(i) The outcomes favorable to the sum of the two numbers being 8 are: (2, 6), (3, 5), (4, 4), (5, 3), (6, 2). Number of favorable outcomes = 5. Therefore, P(sum is 8) = 5/36.
(ii) Since the maximum sum possible is 6 + 6 = 12, all 36 outcomes have a sum less than or equal to 12. Therefore, P(sum ≤ 12) = 36/36 = 1.
